what_it_does: Defines a single ATTACHMENT_TYPE dict declaring a 'docs' attachment type: a documentation file/path bound to a channel (the §3 manifest 'docs' bucket), target_kind='path', multi=True; desc notes such docs are loaded as context for members on join.
observations: target_kind is 'path' — the target is a filesystem path.
References the '§3 manifest docs bucket', tying this to a larger manifest spec.
desc states docs are loaded as context for members on join.
multi=True allows multiple doc paths per channel.
standouts: Docs are framed as join-time context for channel members, not just static attachments.
conventions: Same single-dict descriptor pattern as sibling attachment_types modules.
notes: Registry entry for the 'docs' channel-attachment type: a filesystem path loaded as member join context, many-per-channel.
questions: Where is the §3 manifest defined, and what loads these paths as context on member join?
purpose_vs_actual: Match — declares the docs attachment type as the docstring states.
intention_signals: Aims to let channels carry documentation paths that auto-load as context for anyone joining, declared as data.
novelty: Join-time context loading semantics attached to a plain path target.
contribution: Contributes the declarative definition of the 'docs' attachment type with path target and on-join context-loading semantics.
summary_for_embedding: A descriptor module declaring the 'docs' channel attachment type: a filesystem path (manifest docs bucket) loaded as member join context, multi=True.
intention_for_embedding: To register documentation paths as channel-bound attachments that load as context for members on join.
